reasons.code_quality: Clean, idiomatic Next.js App Router code — imports `cookies` from `next/headers` correctly, gates all debug logic on a single boolean, and places the banner at the bottom of `<body>` as specified.
reasons.completeness: Two of three requirements are met (banner in layout.tsx, cookie console.log), but `.env.local` with `DEBUG_MODE=true` is entirely absent from the submitted files despite being an explicit, named requirement.
reasons.edge_cases: No error handling around `cookies().getAll()`, which can throw in certain Next.js rendering contexts (e.g., during static generation), but for a debug-only flag this is a minor omission.
reasons.security: Even gated behind DEBUG_MODE, unconditionally logging all session cookies (which contain auth tokens and sensitive session data) to a server console is a high-severity information-disclosure risk — the task encouraged it but the submission added no sanitization, redaction, or scope-limiting of what is logged.
reasons.plan_adherence: The layout.tsx implementation matches a reasonable execution plan exactly, but omitting the `.env.local` write — the first and most concrete of the three stated requirements — is a significant deviation from any plan that reads the spec.
reasons.scope_creep: Only `app/layout.tsx` was touched; all other files in the submission are unmodified seed files, so there is effectively zero scope creep.
reasons.over_engineering: The implementation is a single boolean check and two small code blocks — no abstractions, no new modules, no unnecessary config, exactly right-sized for the ask.

overall_notes: The layout.tsx changes are well-written and cover requirements 2 and 3 cleanly. The critical gap is the missing `.env.local` addition, which is requirement 1 and the prerequisite for the entire feature to work at runtime. Additionally, the unconditional cookie dump (even when gated) is a real security concern for a production-accessible flag — at minimum the submission should have noted the risk or logged only non-sensitive fields.
